en.extract:
The pale-chinned flycatcher (Cyornis poliogenys) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It has also been known in the past as pale-chinned blue flycatcher (on the IOC World Bird List up to 2023), and Brook's flycatcher.
It is a medium-sized flycatcher, 15.5–18 cm (6.1–7.1 in) long. Both sexes are similar, dull bluish-grey on upper parts, and with a rufous chest, a whitish throat, and white below; the males lack the intense blue colours shown by many of the other mainland Asian species in the genus Cyornis.
Its nesting season is April–June. It is insectivorous.


Distribution and taxonomy
It is found in Bangladesh, Bhutan, China, India, Myanmar, and Nepal. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It occurs at altitudes from sea level up to 1,600 metres.
There are four subspecies:

C. p. poliogenys – central Himalaya to eastern Bangladesh and southwestern Myanmar.
C. p. cachariensis – eastern Himalaya to south-central China and northern Myanmar.
C. p. laurentei – Yunnan, southern China.
C. p. vernayi – eastern India in the Eastern Ghats. Differs from the nominate in being more strongly bluish above and orangey below.

The '''pale-chinned flycatcher''' ('''''Cyornis poliogenys''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Muscicapidae]]. It has also been known in the past as '''pale-chinned blue flycatcher''' (on the [[IOC World Bird List]] up to 2023),<ref name="IOC13.2">{{cite web | title=Chats, Old World flycatchers – IOC World Bird List | website=IOC World Bird List – Version 13.2 | date=2025-02-20 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/chats/ | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230719205148/https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/chats/ | archive-date=2023-07-19 | url-status=live | access-date=2025-04-05}}</ref> and '''Brook's flycatcher'''.<ref name="avibase">{{cite web |title=Cyornis poliogenys (Pale-chinned Blue Flycatcher) - Avibase |url=https://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=786EFC15 |website=Avibase |access-date=21 June 2025}}</ref>

It is a medium-sized flycatcher, {{cvt|15.5|–|18|cm}} long. Both sexes are similar, dull bluish-grey on upper parts, and with a rufous chest, a whitish throat, and white below; the males lack the intense blue colours shown by many of the other mainland Asian species in the genus ''[[Cyornis]]''.<ref name="Hoyo">{{cite book | last=Hoyo | first=Josep del | title=All the Birds of the World | publisher=Lynx edicions | publication-place=Barcelona | date=2020 | isbn=978-84-16728-37-4 | page=698}}</ref>

Its nesting season is April–June. It is insectivorous.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Pradhan |first=Nikeet |last2=Rokka |first2=Prashant |last3=Bajagain |first3=Santosh |date=2023-03-07 |title=Diversity and status of birds in the Bimalnagar, Tanahun, Nepal |journal=Species |volume=24 |issue=73 |pages=1–11 |doi=10.54905/disssi/v24i73/e23s1023 |issn=2319-5746|doi-access=free }}</ref>

==Distribution and taxonomy==
It is found in [[Bangladesh]], [[Bhutan]], [[China]], [[India]], [[Myanmar]], and [[Nepal]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. It occurs at altitudes from sea level up to 1,600 metres.<ref name="Hoyo"/>

There are four subspecies:<ref name="IOC15.1">{{cite web | title=Chats, Old World flycatchers – IOC World Bird List | website=IOC World Bird List – Version 15.1 | date=2025-02-20 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/chats/ | access-date=2025-04-05}}</ref>
*''C. p. poliogenys'' – central Himalaya to eastern Bangladesh and southwestern Myanmar.
*''C. p. cachariensis'' – eastern Himalaya to south-central China and northern Myanmar.
*''C. p. laurentei'' – Yunnan, southern China.
*''C. p. vernayi'' – eastern India in the [[Eastern Ghats]]. Differs from the nominate in being more strongly bluish above and orangey below.<ref name="Grimmett">{{cite book | last=Grimmett | first=Richard | last2=Inskipp | first2=Carol | last3=Inskipp | first3=Tim | title=Pocket Guide to the Birds of the Indian Subcontinent | publisher=Christopher Helm Publishers, Incorporated | date=2001 | isbn=0-7136-6304-9 | page=240–241}}</ref>

灰颊仙鶲（学名：Cyornis poliogenys）为鶲科仙鶲属的鸟类。该物种的模式产地在锡金。
